variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_crt/crt.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is K (number of equations, >= 1). Next K lines are 'r m' pairs (remainder and modulus, m >= 1, 0 <= r < m). Output the smallest non-negative integer x satisfying x ≡ r_i (mod m_i) for all i, or -1 if no solution exists. Use general CRT (handles non-coprime moduli — gcd(m_i, m_j) need not be 1, but the remainders must be consistent on the gcd). Use 64-bit signed arithmetic; all answers fit in i64. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_crt; python3 crt.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_crt/crt.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is K (number of equations, >= 1). Next K lines are 'r m' pairs (remainder and modulus, m >= 1, 0 <= r < m). Output the smallest non-negative integer x satisfying x ≡ r_i (mod m_i) for all i, or -1 if no solution exists. Use general CRT (handles non-coprime moduli — gcd(m_i, m_j) need not be 1, but the remainders must be consistent on the gcd). Use 64-bit signed arithmetic; all answers fit in i64. Compile with `go build -o crt crt.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_crt; go build -o crt crt.go && ./crt < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

Use general CRT (handles non-coprime moduli — gcd(m_i, m_j) need not be 1, but the remainders must be consistent on the gcd). Use 64-bit signed arithmetic; all answers fit in i64. variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_crt/crt.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is K (number of equations, >= 1). Next K lines are 'r m' pairs (remainder and modulus, m >= 1, 0 <= r < m). Output the smallest non-negative integer x satisfying x ≡ r_i (mod m_i) for all i, or -1 if no solution exists. Use general CRT (handles non-coprime moduli — gcd(m_i, m_j) need not be 1, but the remainders must be consistent on the gcd). Use 64-bit signed arithmetic; all answers fit in i64. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast crt.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_crt; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast crt.zig && ./crt < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
